mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns dominate in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production expands faster than chained GDP during supply-constrained regimes, inflationary pressures force monetary tightening and suppress S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth relative to GDPC1 correlates with positive SPX returns in supply-constrained periods over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.3 percent signals accelerating inflation, prompting immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day SPX returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive divergence where RSAFS growth exceeds GDPC1 growth yields positive SPX returns in >70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below negative 0.5 percent indicates immediate supply-side contraction and manufacturing weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5 percent MoM.
